meta_title: Impact Doors in Greenacres, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Greenacres, FL. ESW & CWI brands, in-house crews, $0 down financing. Protect your home & save on insurance.
h1: Greenacres' Trusted Source for Hurricane-Rated Impact Doors That Actually Protect Your Home
intro:
Greenacres, Florida sits roughly 12 miles inland from the Atlantic coast, but don't let that distance give you a false sense of security. When a major hurricane makes landfall along South Florida's coastline, wind speeds in inland Palm Beach County communities like Greenacres can still reach destructive levels — and your front door, back sliding glass door, or French doors are often the first points of failure. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, known throughout Palm Beach County as 'The Kings of Service,' specializes in installing hurricane-rated impact doors that are engineered to hold the line when it matters most.

Homeowners throughout Greenacres — from the established single-family neighborhoods of Acreage Pines and Limetree to the active adult community of Palm Beach Leisureville — are increasingly choosing impact doors not just for storm protection, but for the year-round benefits they bring. Everyday advantages like noise reduction, enhanced energy efficiency, and upgraded home security make impact doors a smart investment regardless of hurricane season. With the average Greenacres home valued at approximately $310,000, protecting that investment with code-compliant, high-performance impact doors is one of the most financially sound decisions a homeowner can make.

Approximately 40% of homes in Greenacres were built before 1994 — the year Florida's building codes were significantly tightened following Hurricane Andrew's catastrophic impact. Many of these older homes are still fitted with standard hollow-core entry doors, aluminum sliders, or single-pane French doors that offer virtually no meaningful wind resistance. Under Palm Beach County's current wind zone requirements of 140 mph, every door opening in your home must be protected with a rated product. Whether you're upgrading a primary entry door, a rear sliding glass door leading to a patio, or a set of decorative French doors, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has the products, the expertise, and the local knowledge to get the job done right — fully permitted and inspected through the City of Greenacres Building Department.

section_why.h2: Why Greenacres Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Door Upgrades
section_why.body:
At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we don't subcontract your project out to whoever is available that week. Every impact door installation we complete in Greenacres is handled entirely by our own in-house crew — experienced technicians who have installed doors in hundreds of homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties. With more than 60 years of combined installation experience on our team, we bring a level of consistency and craftsmanship that fly-by-night contractors simply can't match. When you call Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you get the same quality on the last door we install as you do on the first.

We understand the specific construction characteristics of Greenacres homes. Older block construction homes in neighborhoods like Limetree and Acreage Pines often require customized rough opening adjustments that a less experienced crew might overlook. Pre-1994 homes may have non-standard door framing, deteriorating sills, or outdated threshold configurations that need to be addressed before a new impact door can be properly seated and sealed. Our installation teams are trained to identify and resolve these issues on-site, ensuring that your new door not only looks great but performs exactly as it was rated to perform under high-wind conditions.

Our A+ Better Business Bureau rating and 500+ completed homes across South Florida speak to a track record that Greenacres homeowners can rely on. We handle every aspect of your project from the initial in-home consultation and product selection through permit submission to the City of Greenacres Building Department, professional installation, and final inspection scheduling. You never have to chase down a permit status or wonder whether your installation passed code — we manage all of it on your behalf.

We also recognize that Greenacres is a community of hardworking families and value-conscious homeowners who want the best product at a fair price. That's why we offer flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down available for qualified applicants, so you don't have to wait until hurricane season is already bearing down to make this critical upgrade. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is committed to making premium impact door protection accessible to every homeowner in the Greenacres area.

section_history.h2: Greenacres Has Seen the Damage — Here's What Storm History Tells Us About Door Protection
section_history.body:
Greenacres and the surrounding Palm Beach County communities have faced significant hurricane threats seven times since 1990, with three storms standing out as particularly instructive for homeowners thinking about impact door upgrades. Hurricane Frances in 2004 was a slow-moving, prolonged storm that subjected South Florida to sustained damaging winds and heavy rain for hours — the kind of extended battering that exposed every weakness in older door frames and inadequate door seals. Homes throughout Palm Beach County reported water intrusion around entry doors and sliding glass door tracks, causing interior damage that far exceeded the cost of proper impact door installation.

Just one year later, Hurricane Wilma tore across South Florida in October 2005 with remarkable speed and ferocity, producing some of the highest wind gusts ever recorded in Palm Beach County. Wilma was a harsh reminder that hurricane season threats don't diminish as storms cross from the Gulf Coast to the Atlantic side — they simply change character. Communities like Greenacres, Wellington, and Royal Palm Beach experienced widespread damage to standard residential doors, with many hinges, frames, and locking mechanisms failing under pressure. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which brushed Palm Beach County's western communities and once again demonstrated that homes without impact-rated openings were far more vulnerable to wind-driven debris and pressure differential failures.

That pattern of recurring storm exposure is exactly why the State of Florida and Palm Beach County enforce strict wind-rated product requirements. Each storm has reinforced the same lesson: standard doors are not sufficient, and the cost of repairs after a storm is almost always higher than the cost of upgrading to impact doors before one arrives.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code and Palm Beach County Requirements for Impact Doors in Greenacres
section_code.body:
Greenacres falls within Palm Beach County's wind zone designation requiring products rated to withstand 140 mph wind speeds. While the city is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a stricter designation that applies to Miami-Dade and Broward counties — Greenacres homeowners are still required under the Florida Building Code to install hurricane-rated impact products for any new door installation or replacement. This applies to entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors alike. Products must carry an approved Florida Product Approval number, confirming they have passed independent laboratory testing for large and small missile impact resistance as well as pressure cycling.

All impact door installations in Greenacres require a building permit issued through the City of Greenacres Building Department. This is not a bureaucratic formality — it's a critical consumer protection measure. A permitted installation is inspected by a licensed building official who verifies that the door was installed correctly, the rough opening was properly prepared, the anchoring meets structural requirements, and the installation matches the product's Florida Product Approval specifications. Unpermitted impact door installations not only fail to comply with code but can also void your product warranty and create complications when filing an insurance wind mitigation report or during a future home sale.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permit process for every Greenacres installation we complete. We submit your permit application, coordinate with the City of Greenacres Building Department, schedule the required inspections, and provide you with documentation confirming your installation was completed to code. For homeowners in older pre-1994 neighborhoods like those found throughout ZIP codes 33413, 33415, 33463, and 33467, this full-service permitting approach ensures your investment is fully protected from a regulatory standpoint.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: The Brands Premier Impact Windows & Roofing Trusts for Greenacres Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact doors from two industry-leading brands: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI — both of which carry Florida Product Approvals and are engineered specifically for the South Florida climate. ESW's impact door line includes heavy-duty entry door systems, sliding glass doors, and French door configurations built with reinforced aluminum frames, multi-point locking systems, and laminated impact-resistant glass. The multi-point locking mechanism is a particularly important feature for Greenacres homeowners — rather than securing at a single point like a standard deadbolt, these systems engage the door frame at three or more points simultaneously, dramatically improving resistance to both forced entry and wind pressure.

CWI's product lineup complements ESW with additional configurations designed to meet a wide range of architectural styles found across Greenacres neighborhoods. Whether you're looking for a traditional raised-panel entry door for a classic block home in Limetree, a large-format sliding glass door for a patio opening in Palm Beach Leisureville, or an elegant French door set for a newer home in Acreage Pines, CWI offers durable, code-compliant options that don't sacrifice aesthetics for performance. Both ESW and CWI products are rated for 140 mph wind loads, ensuring full compliance with Palm Beach County's requirements without the more stringent HVHZ protocols that apply in coastal counties.

Beyond hurricane protection, both brands deliver measurable everyday performance benefits. The laminated glass used in impact doors acts as a powerful sound barrier — a real advantage for homes near high-traffic corridors like Lake Worth Road or Jog Road. The tight seals and thermal breaks built into ESW and CWI door frames also reduce air infiltration, helping to maintain consistent indoor temperatures and reduce the load on your air conditioning system during Florida's long, hot summers.

section_cost.h2: What Greenacres Homeowners Can Expect to Invest in Impact Door Upgrades
section_cost.body:
The cost of impact door installation in Greenacres varies depending on the type, size, and quantity of doors being replaced. A single impact-rated entry door installation typically ranges from $1,500 to $3,500 including materials and labor, while sliding glass door replacements — particularly larger configurations — may range from $2,500 to $5,000 or more. French door systems, especially those with sidelights or transoms, can fall at the higher end of the range. For a home with an average value of $310,000, these investments represent a relatively modest percentage of total home value while delivering protection and efficiency benefits that compound over time.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes impact door upgrades accessible for Greenacres homeowners through flexible financing options with payments starting at just $87 per month and $0 down programs available to qualified buyers. Rather than depleting savings or waiting until after a storm forces an emergency replacement, financing allows you to upgrade now — before hurricane season — and spread the cost over time in a way that fits your household budget. For a lower-middle income community like Greenacres, this kind of accessible financing can make the difference between getting protected and staying exposed.

The return on investment for impact doors extends well beyond storm season. Greenacres homeowners who upgrade their doors typically see meaningful reductions in their homeowners insurance premiums through wind mitigation credits, a topic covered in more detail in the next section. Energy savings from improved sealing and insulation can further offset costs over the life of the product. And because impact doors carry long product warranties and require minimal maintenance compared to wooden or standard aluminum doors, the long-term cost of ownership is often lower than continuing to live with aging, underperforming door systems.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings and Wind Mitigation Benefits for Greenacres Impact Door Owners
section_insurance.body:
One of the most compelling financial arguments for upgrading to impact doors in Greenacres is the potential for significant homeowners insurance savings. Florida law requires insurance carriers to offer premium discounts to homeowners who install wind mitigation features — and impact-rated doors are among the qualifying improvements. When a licensed wind mitigation inspector visits your home following your impact door installation, they document the product ratings and installation method on a standard OIR-B1-1802 Wind Mitigation Inspection form. This report is submitted to your insurance carrier and can unlock credits that reduce your annual premium, often by hundreds of dollars per year.

Major insurers operating in Palm Beach County — including Citizens Property Insurance Corporation and numerous private carriers — recognize impact door upgrades as a risk-reduction measure and price their policies accordingly. For Greenacres homeowners with older homes that may still have original non-impact doors, the premium difference between an unprotected property and a fully protected one can be substantial. In some cases, the annual insurance savings alone can effectively cover a significant portion of the monthly financing payment on a new impact door system, making the upgrade nearly self-financing over time.

It's important to note that to qualify for wind mitigation credits related to your door openings, all exterior openings — including windows — typically need to be impact-rated or covered by approved shutters. Many Greenacres homeowners are bundling impact door and window installations together to maximize their wind mitigation score and capture the highest possible insurance discount. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing can help you evaluate your home's current protection status and develop an upgrade plan that delivers the most insurance and storm protection benefit for your investment.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors in Greenacres if I'm 12 miles from the coast?
faqs.a: Yes. Inland location does not reduce your obligation under the Florida Building Code or your exposure to hurricane-force winds. Greenacres sits in a 140 mph wind zone, and storms like Frances, Wilma, and Irma all produced damaging winds well inland across Palm Beach County. Impact doors are required for any door replacement or new installation under current code, and they provide critical protection regardless of coastal proximity.

faqs.q: How much does impact door installation cost in Greenacres, and do you offer financing?
faqs.a: Impact door costs in Greenacres typically range from $1,500 to $5,000 or more per opening depending on door type, size, and configuration. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners. Our team provides detailed, transparent quotes after an in-home consultation so you know exactly what to expect before committing.

faqs.q: Does impact door installation in Greenacres require a permit?
faqs.a: Yes. All impact door installations in Greenacres require a building permit issued through the City of Greenacres Building Department. This ensures your installation is inspected and confirmed to meet Florida Building Code requirements for the 140 mph wind zone. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf, from application to final inspection, so you don't have to navigate the process alone.

faqs.q: How long does it take to have impact doors installed in Greenacres?
faqs.a: After your in-home consultation and product selection, the typical timeline from contract signing to completed installation is four to eight weeks, depending on product lead times and permit processing at the City of Greenacres Building Department. The physical installation of most door configurations takes one to two days. Our team keeps you informed at every stage of the process so there are no surprises.

faqs.q: Will new impact doors lower my homeowners insurance in Greenacres?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes. Florida law requires insurers to offer wind mitigation credits for homes with impact-rated openings. After installation, a licensed wind mitigation inspector can document your new doors on a state-recognized form that you submit to your insurance carrier. Many Greenacres homeowners see annual premium reductions of several hundred dollars, which helps offset the cost of the upgrade over time. Bundling impact doors with impact windows typically maximizes the available credit.
